why: A non-`default` stack MUST round-trip into the 6-segment stack-aware form. Guards the sibling of cortex#1812 (a real stack must not be lost).
public. subjects carry NEITHER principal NOR stack; the identity fields MUST be ignored (specs/namespace.md:57-59; deriveSubject :618-620).
With stack omitted (legacy opt-in) the 5-segment migration form is emitted. This form is subject-matching compat ONLY (§8, OD-2).

MASKING CASE. With no hint the wire form is undecidable (stack=default,type=tasks.chat) vs (type=default.tasks.chat); detectSubjectForm defaults to `legacy` and the fabricated `default` coincidentally matches — the exact silent failure that produced cortex#1812's `default`-derivation defect.
The out-of-band stack hint resolves the §3.3 ambiguity to stack-aware. Precision on the stack-aware path REQUIRES a hint (src/subjects.ts:799-801).

ROOT CAUSE cortex#1812. The default-derivation SHOULD is subject-matching-only; an absent stack MUST NOT be fabricated into `default` for identity resolution / roster / stack-id (§8). Fault, never a default.
Baseline DID→@-segment render: ':' → '-' (specs/namespace.md:209-213; src/subjects.ts:128).
COLLISION PAIR (half 1). '.' → '--'. Must stay DISTINCT from did:mf:hub-metafactory below — the collision that was 'a real security boundary violation' (specs/namespace.md:236).
COLLISION PAIR (half 2). '-' preserved. Distinct from the '.' case above; the '.'/'-' distinction is the injectivity guarantee for this pair.

NON-INJECTIVITY COLLISION (half 1). did:mf:a-.b and did:mf:a.-b are BOTH DID_RE-valid and BOTH encode to @did-mf-a---b — the spec's injectivity claim is FALSE for '-.'/'.-' adjacency (audit provenance/assistant-segment-encoding-collision). Gated on OD-1 / cortex#1880.

NON-INJECTIVITY COLLISION (half 2). Encodes byte-identically to did:mf:a-.b — two distinct assistants become one wire address. The '---' run is ambiguous ('-'+'--' vs '--'+'-'). Gated on OD-1 / cortex#1880.

The @-segment grammar is @[a-z][a-z0-9-]* — '_' is NOT in the charset. DEFECT: encodeDidSegment('did:mf:a_b') PRODUCES this invalid segment because DID_RE admits '_' but the encoder does not rewrite it (audit subjects/did-underscore-leaks-into-at-segment). Co-owned with RFC-0001, OD-1.
Seed-taxonomy tag: single interior hyphen, ends alnum, 2–64 chars — valid under CAPABILITY_TAG_RE (src/patterns.ts:21).

CAPABILITY_TAG_RE requires >=2 chars; single-char tags collide with 1-char structured identifiers. But taskSubject/offerTaskSubject accept 'x' via STACK_SEGMENT_REGEX (1-char legal) — emitter-dependent validity (FINDING §7.1).
CAPABILITY_TAG_RE bans trailing '-'; STACK_SEGMENT_REGEX permits it. 'scan-' is valid to taskSubject but invalid to bidRequestSubject — the three-way capability drift (FINDING §7.1).

specs/namespace.md:172-174 says a capability tag equal to 'dead-letter' is a publish-time validation error. DEFECT: no code rejects it — 'dead-letter' matches CAPABILITY_TAG_RE and taskDeadLetterSubject merely no-ops (src/subjects.ts:529-531), so escalation subjects are spoofable (FINDING §7.3).

Published subjects MUST be fully qualified (specs/namespace.md:130). DEFECT: subjectFor/deriveSubject skip segment validation on principal/type and return 'local.*.x.y' (audit subjects/derive-helpers-skip-grammar-validation, FINDING §7.3).
Stack-aware dead-letter derivation MUST preserve the stack and the first capability segment (src/subjects.ts:508-535).

COLLISION: a stack literally named 'tasks'. DEFECT: taskDeadLetterSubject checks parts[2]==='tasks' with LEGACY priority, misparsing the stack-aware subject as legacy and dropping the stack (audit subjects/stack-named-tasks-misparse, FINDING §7.8). Vector states the correct expectation.

Segments are always lowercase (specs/namespace.md:107). DEFECT: sanitizeSubjectToken preserves A–Z, emitting 'local.acme._metrics.transport.MyApp' (audit subjects/metrics-subject-uppercase-leak, FINDING §7.7).
Prefix↔classification alignment is the ONLY subject↔envelope check implemented (src/subjects.ts:703-722); a runtime guard, not a grammar property (FINDING §7.9 / SEC).
A prefix/classification mismatch is a protocol violation (specs/namespace.md:341). Enforced by a runtime transport guard, not by the subject format.

Total subject MUST be <=255 chars (specs/namespace.md:111). DEFECT: no code path measures composed length anywhere in src/ (audit subjects/total-255-cap-unenforced, FINDING §7.6); over-long subjects surface as opaque NATS errors.
